title: branch — realtime broadcasts
description: The branch module publishes one broadcast channel — `tenant.{id}.branches` — for the SPA's live branch list. Domain events (BranchCreated, BranchStatusChanged, BranchOpeningHoursUpdated, BranchCoordinatesUpdated) fan out to this channel so admins see updates without polling. High-frequency events (like every capacity change in a busy tenant) are debounced 5 seconds to avoid channel spam.

channels.name: tenant.{id}.branches
channels.authorization: authenticated tenant member with `branches.viewAny` on the tenant
channels.purpose: Live updates for the branch list + map view + admin dashboards. Consumed by the SDUI branch list screen + the tenant-admin dashboard.

events_broadcast.payload_shape: { branch_id, organization_id, region_id, name, slug, is_default }

events_broadcast.debounce_note: Debounced so bulk edits don't flood the channel — the final state broadcasts once per branch per 2-second window.

events_broadcast.note: Coordinates lat/lng are NOT included in the broadcast payload — competitive-intel risk (unless the channel-listener explicitly re-fetches via GET /branches/{branch}).

notes: The channel is tenant-scoped (`tenant.{id}.branches`), not org-scoped. A tenant with multiple organizations receives updates for every branch across every org on the single channel — SPA client-side filtering handles the per-org view.
`branch.deleted` is NOT broadcast — public API never hard-deletes; the platform-support CLI path bypasses broadcast because the tenant is being erased alongside.
Sensitive branch fields (coordinates lat/lng, capacity exact value, phone) are NEVER in the broadcast payload — listeners re-fetch via the REST API when they need the full row.
The Wave 3 attendance / booking modules will add their own channels (`tenant.{id}.branch.{branch_id}.bookings`) for per-branch live occupancy — those are OWNED by those modules, not this one.
